channel.title: Morning Money
channel.description: Delivered daily by 8 a.m., Morning Money examines the latest news in finance, politics and policy — and on Fridays, Morning Money: Capital Risk highlights the downstream effects on markets.

item.title: The Calvin Coolidge of Fed chairs?
item.description: Consider the shocks that markets endured in the second year of President Donald Trump’s second term.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: How Home Depot sees the housing freeze
item.description: Home Depot wants to see the doers in Congress get more done when it comes to reviving the housing market.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: Trump enters the octagon at the G7
item.description: Don’t set grand expectations for major breakthroughs at the G7.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: Washington frets over ‘The Little Engine that Could’
item.description: Wall Street, the cryptocurrency industry and farmers alike always expected the Commodity Futures Trading Commission — a tiny but powerful financial regulator rooted in the agricultural markets — to take on new prominence under President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: By Declan Harty

item.title: The AI boom is pushing up inflation
item.description: Most Americans think President Donald Trump’s tariffs led to higher prices.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: The Trump $250 bill struggles on the Hill
item.description: Republicans are having a hard time getting excited about printing a $250 bill with President Donald Trump on it.
item.dc:creator: By Jasper Goodman

item.title: The World According to French
item.description: House Financial Services Chair French Hill is close to pulling off the impossible.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: The bright spot in Trump’s economy
item.description: A lot of things haven’t gone right for President Donald Trump over the last six months.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: Blockbuster IPOs could give Trump an irresistible market story
item.description: President Donald Trump loves a market success story — especially when he can claim it as his own.
item.dc:creator: By Yasmin Khorram

item.title: Voters fear AI. Unions want the jobs.
item.description: Big labor unions are all-in on a multi-trillion dollar AI infrastructure boom.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: 'A new world’: The banks’ final push on crypto
item.description: Wall Street is preparing to make its last stand against crypto.
item.dc:creator: By Declan Harty

item.title: Nice AI story. Be a shame if shareholders… fact-checked it.
item.description: It isn’t a big stretch to assume regulators will take a dim view of companies that use terms like “automation” and “innovation” to elide challenges that may be material to investors.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: Debanking politics and the midterms
item.description: President Donald Trump knows what it feels like to be “debanked” by powerful financial institutions.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton and Michael Stratford
item.dc:contributor: By Sam Sutton and Michael Stratford

item.title: No strait line for inflation relief
item.description: There is an expectation that AI will make businesses and workers more productive in the long run. But the near-term outlook suggests prices will continue to climb.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: A housing win?
item.description: The House’s passage of housing legislation marked the second time a version of the bill has cleared a chamber of Congress with a bipartisan blowout. But it’s not clear whether this is the combination that will stick.
item.dc:creator: By Katherine Hapgood and Sam Sutton
item.dc:contributor: By Katherine Hapgood and Sam Sutton

item.title: ‘Hard to avoid the scrutiny:’ Congress takes on prediction markets
item.description: A sprawling legal and political fight over prediction markets has already taken over Wall Street, courthouses across the country and the mainstream media.
item.dc:creator: By Declan Harty

item.title: A strange coalition
item.description: The fight over cryptocurrency and its proper place in the regulated financial system has brought together a disparate bunch of interests.
item.dc:creator: By Aiden Reiter

item.title: America’s confounding consumer
item.description: Corporate America’s blockbuster earnings season may be about to run into a reality check.
item.dc:creator: By Declan Harty

item.title: Crypto’s moment of truth in the Senate
item.description: One of the biggest financial policy fights Congress has seen in years is set to come to a head this morning in the Senate Banking Committee.
item.dc:creator: By Jasper Goodman and Sam Sutton
item.dc:contributor: By Jasper Goodman and Sam Sutton

item.title: Warsh has a Democrat problem
item.description: Kevin Warsh is about to succeed Jerome Powell as chair of the Federal Reserve. As it stands, there’s one Democrat in his corner.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: Where the money is
item.description: If you want to understand how the world has changed since President Donald Trump first rose to power, follow the money.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: A letter from Warshville
item.description: Kevin Warsh is days away from clinching the chairmanship of the Federal Reserve. So, how many days until President Donald Trump gets mad at him?
item.dc:creator: By Victoria Guida and Sam Sutton
item.dc:contributor: By Victoria Guida and Sam Sutton

item.title: Milken vs. Reality
item.description: When it comes to how Wall Street and Silicon Valley kingmakers view the U.S. economy, it’s all systems go.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton, Yasmin Khorram and Declan Harty
item.dc:contributor: By Sam Sutton, Yasmin Khorram and Declan Harty

item.title: The Rahm Show
item.description: According to Rahm Emanuel, the biggest challenge both parties faced over the last quarter-century has been the battle between establishment forces and anti-establishment forces.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton, Yasmin Khorram and Declan Harty
item.dc:contributor: By Sam Sutton, Yasmin Khorram and Declan Harty

item.title: AI bulls on parade
item.description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang's upbeat take on AI’s economic impact hasn’t mattered a lick when it comes to public opinion.
item.dc:creator: By Yasmin Khorram, Sam Sutton and Michael Stratford
item.dc:contributor: By Yasmin Khorram, Sam Sutton and Michael Stratford

item.title: ‘Constant rebuild mode’
item.description: The fragmentation of trade and security alliances that support Western economies has weighed on CEOs just as it has for global policymakers.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: ‘That’s not over’: Powell’s final warning
item.description: Kevin Warsh has his work cut out for him if he wants to deliver the lower rates that Trump wants.
item.dc:creator: By Victoria Guida and Declan Harty
item.dc:contributor: By Victoria Guida and Declan Harty

item.title: Passing the baton on inflation
item.description: Kevin Warsh runs the risk of misreading the upside risks to inflation if he’s overly reliant on his preferred metric.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: It’s Trump’s economy. And he’s got a Biden-era playbook to fix it.
item.description: President Donald Trump is fond of saying that he inherited a mess from Joe Biden.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton

item.title: An ethical tipping point?
item.description: When anything can become a market, anyone can become a market manipulator.
item.dc:creator: By Sam Sutton
